Title: Combined Transcriptomic and Metabolomic Analysis Reveals an Ethylene-Activated Regulatory Model on Monoterpenoid Indole Alkaloid Biosynthesis in Catharanthus roseus

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive alkaloids effective in treating cancer and other diseases
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes iridoid-pathway gene expression and, together with CrEIL1, synergistically increases secologanin and downstream MIAs including anhydrovinblastine and vinblastine
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BIS2 physically interacts with CrEIL1 in vitro and in vivo. 2. BIS2 alone activates 8-HGO, 7-DLGT and 7-DLH promoters. 3. Co-overexpression with CrEIL1 further elevates iridoid-pathway transcripts and MIA accumulation beyond either gene alone.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; pull-down; co-IP; dual-LUC; transient overexpression in petals; LC-MS metabolite profiling; RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Co-expression with CrEIL1 is suggested as a more potent engineering strategy than single-gene expression to enhance MIA production
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although BIS2 is a transcription factor, in this paper it is an interacting partner rather than one of the two principal ethylene TF subjects; classified here as Common_Gene per core-study emphasis.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive alkaloids effective in treating cancer and other diseases
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Downstream MIA pathway gateway step after strictosidine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes strictosidine to its deglycosylated aglycone, enabling downstream conversion to diverse MIAs
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for downstream formation of catharanthine, ajmalicine, anhydrovinblastine and vinblastine
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control gateway enzyme targeted directly by CrEIN3
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activation of SGD promotes flux toward downstream MIAs and contributes to increased vinblastine/anhydrovinblastine accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SGD promoter is directly bound and activated by CrEIN3 through an ATGTA-box. 2. SGD expression increases in CrEIN3-OE and decreases in CrEIN3- or CrEIL1-silenced tissues. 3. SGD is highlighted as a gateway enzyme for ethylene-enhanced downstream MIA production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: dual-LUC; Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; overexpression/silencing with RT-qPCR and metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Responds to both ETH and MJ; sustained ETH induction
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A key enzyme candidate for heterologous engineering and stacking with CrEIN3/CrEIL1 to boost MIA production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study emphasizes direct CrEIN3-dependent transcriptional control of SGD as a central ethylene regulatory mechanism.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive alkaloids effective in treating cancer and other diseases
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Downstream MIA biosynthesis and flux toward bisindole alkaloids
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of biosynthetic genes and activator TFs; repression of repressor TF genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SGD; ORCA3; SAT; CS; TS; PRX1; GBF1; ZCT1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates ORCA3, SGD, SAT, CS, TS and PRX1; represses GBF1 and ZCT1; increases CrERF5 expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of vinblastine, anhydrovinblastine, ajmalicine and catharanthine; required for ETH-induced MIA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key ethylene-signaling mediator and major direct activator of the gateway enzyme SGD for downstream MIA production
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No specific protein complex demonstrated; acts directly on SGD promoter
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in flowers and old leaves
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced under ethylene-responsive modules (pink/green); associated with catharanthine and anhydrovinblastine networks
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in transient overexpression and ChIP assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Arabidopsis ein3 eil1 complementation; VIGS; transient overexpression; dual-LUC; Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; RT-qPCR; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate regulator for engineering enhanced bisindole MIA production; can be stacked with SGD or CrEIL1/BIS2 modules in plant or yeast platforms
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrEIN3 is an ethylene-responsive nuclear EIN3/EIL TF identified from integrated transcriptome-metabolome networks. 2. Overexpression raises, while silencing lowers, MIA-pathway gene expression and alkaloid accumulation. 3. CrEIN3 directly binds the SGD promoter ATGTA-box in Y1H, EMSA and ChIP-qPCR, establishing a direct ethylene-to-SGD regulatory route. 4. It also promotes ORCA3/CrERF5 and represses GBF1/ZCT1, reinforcing MIA flux toward vinblastine and anhydrovinblastine.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive alkaloids effective in treating cancer and other diseases
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Upstream iridoid pathway activation and broader MIA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Indirect transcriptional activation via interaction with BIS2; activation of activator TFs and repression of repressor TF genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Ethylene/ethephon signal; integrates with JA-responsive BIS2
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: BIS2-associated targets including DXS, G8O, 8-HGO, 7-DLGT, 7-DLH, LAMT, T16H1, ORCA3; also TDC, STR, SGD, SAT, D4H, DAT, PRX1; GBF1; ZCT1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates ORCA3, BIS2, CrWRKY1, CrERF5 and multiple iridoid/indole/vindoline/MIA genes; represses GBF1 and ZCT1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of secologanin, vindoline, ajmalicine, anhydrovinblastine and vinblastine; required for ETH-induced MIA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key ethylene-signaling mediator that cooperates with BIS2 to strengthen upstream iridoid supply for downstream bisindole MIA production
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions through physical interaction with BIS2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in young leaves and flowers
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ethylene/JA-responsive turquoise-module regulator associated with vindoline and iridoid genes
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in transient overexpression/co-expression assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Arabidopsis ein3 eil1 complementation; VIGS; transient overexpression; dual-LUC; Y2H; BiFC; pull-down; co-IP; RT-qPCR; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target, especially in co-expression with BIS2, to increase precursor supply and high-value MIA output in plant or yeast systems
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrEIL1 is a nuclear EIN3/EIL TF identified as a key ethylene mediator linked to iridoid and vindoline gene modules. 2. Overexpression elevates, while silencing reduces, secologanin and multiple downstream MIAs. 3. CrEIL1 does not directly bind tested target promoters but physically interacts with JA-responsive BIS2. 4. The CrEIL1-BIS2 interaction synergistically enhances activation of iridoid-pathway genes and strongly boosts anhydrovinblastine and vinblastine accumulation.
